---
title: "Record Management In Workspace"
---

---
title: "Record Management in Workspace"
source: "https://support.hyland.com/r/Current/Hyland-Governance/wtj1748330034769"
section: "Hyland Governance"
---

Governance records in Workspace hold the metadata that allow them to match the relevant documents in the content repository. In the Governance section, you can manage records that are created based on your repository and retention settings configured in Studio Modeling.
To manage records in Workspace:

1.  Open Workspace.
2.  Click the  Governance  tab on the left pane.   ![The Governance tab in Workspace](data:image/png;base64,)  Note: The  Governance  tab in Workspace is available only for users with Record Manager permissions.  The records search view is displayed.
3.  Filter the records:
1. Click the filter you want to use. For the description of the available filters, see Filtering Records.Tip: The filters correspond with the tabs when creating a retention policy. See the Creating a Retention Policy topic.
2. Expand the filter and select the appropriate item from the list.
3. Click Apply.
The results of your search are displayed:
-  Record Name represents the recorded document.
-  Category displays the names of the categories.
-  Data Source displays the name of the source repository.
-  Cutoff date displays the date when the retention begins.
-  Disposition Date displays the duration for which the content will be retained. If in the process of creating the retention policy:
-  Retain content for a fixed period of is selected, a date until when the content will be retained is displayed.
-  Retain content until retention adjusted is selected, the record in Governance displays Waiting for adjustment message. When the property date is reached, this message is replaced with the date. If there is an extra retention time after trigger selected when creating a retention policy, the disposition date is calculated according to the selected period of time.
-  Disposition displays the action that will be performed on the recorded document after the retention period.
-  Status displays the current stage of the record within the Governance project.
4.  On the Records Management tab, you can perform all or any of the following actions:
- To change the number of items displayed on each page of the document list, select a different number in the Items per page field in the lower-left corner.
- To jump to a different page in the document list, click the icon of the target page in the lower-right corner of the list: first page, previous page, next page, or last page.
- To view the properties of a record, select a record and click the property button. ![A property button.](data:image/png;base64,)
- To add a record to a legal hold case, select a record and click the Add selected records to a legal hold icon. !['Add Records to a legal hold' icon](data:image/png;base64,)
- To reset the filtering results, click the Reset button.
